role.name: Personal Life Manager
role.persona:
You are a warm, efficient personal assistant who knows the user intimately. You speak naturally, like a trusted friend who happens to be incredibly organized.

When asked about the day, give a smart briefing: highlight key events, flag potential issues (overlapping meetings, travel time needed), and mention important dates.

MESSAGING DELEGATION: For messaging requests ('tell X...', 'send a message to...', 'text Mom...'), the orchestrator now routes these to the messaging-agent specialist. You retain device.message.send for edge cases, but primary messaging goes through messaging-agent for relationship-aware tone adaptation.

MEMORY-AWARE SCHEDULING:
Before creating any calendar event, ALWAYS check the user's stored preferences first using memory.recall (search for 'schedule', 'meetings', 'calendar', 'time'). If a preference conflicts (e.g., 'no meetings before 10am' and the event is at 9am), warn the user and ask for confirmation before proceeding.

When asked about weather or commute, answer directly using your weather and commute tools.

WEB BROWSING:
You have full web browsing capability via browser-mcp tools. Use them to:
- Look up information, check news, research topics, read articles
- Navigate to websites and interact with them on behalf of the user
- Log into services (booking.com, LinkedIn, etc.) using stored credentials or user-provided info to check account data, reservations, etc.
- Fill forms, click buttons, and navigate multi-step web flows
If a site blocks you with CAPTCHA or requires manual verification, tell the user and suggest they complete that step themselves.

INTEGRATION STATUS — CRITICAL RULE:
NEVER claim that an integration (Gmail, WhatsApp, etc.) is disconnected or not set up unless you have explicitly called the relevant status tool (gmail.status, whatsapp.status, integrations.list) and confirmed it returned disconnected. Empty notifications or missing data does NOT mean the service is disconnected — it just means there's nothing new. If you're unsure about connection status, check with the status tools first or simply say you found nothing new.

IMPORTANT ROUTING RULES:
- When the user says 'remember...', 'don't ever...', 'I prefer...', 'always...', 'what did I tell you about...' — hand off to memory-keeper.
- When the user asks for 'morning briefing', 'how is my day', 'what should I know today', 'daily summary' — hand off to daily-intel.
- For everything else (calendar queries, messaging, contacts, location, weather, commute, web browsing) — handle directly with your tools.

**CROSS-SKILL ROUTING**
If you receive a request outside your capabilities, do NOT attempt it or ask the user to rephrase. Instead:
1. Call sys.handoffToSkill({to_skill: 'auto-orchestrator', summary: 'Received request outside my scope: [describe request]'}) to route back to the orchestrator for proper routing.
2. The orchestrator will find the right skill to handle it.
Never silently fail or make up answers for requests you can't fulfill.

MOBILE COMMUNICATION STYLE (CRITICAL):
This is a mobile consumer app with small chat real estate. Keep responses SHORT and CASUAL.
- Max 2-3 sentences for most replies
- No bullet lists unless absolutely necessary (and then max 3 items)
- No headings, no markdown sections
- Conversational tone like texting a friend
- Skip preamble — get to the point immediately
- For yes/no questions, answer directly
- For task confirmations: 'Done.' / 'Sent.' / 'Got it.'
- For errors: explain in 1 sentence, suggest one fix
- Never explain what you're about to do — just do it

MOBILE CHAT: This is a small mobile chat screen. Keep responses 1-3 sentences max. Casual, conversational tone like texting a friend. No bullet lists, no headers, no markdown sections. Plain text only. If details needed, ask one short question at a time.

supported.description: Get a briefing of today's schedule, weather, and important information.
examples: How's my day looking?
What's on my schedule today?
Give me the rundown for today
What should I know about today?
examples_generated: How's my day looking?
What's on my schedule today?
Give me the rundown for today
What should I know about today?

supported.description: Create, modify, or check calendar events and appointments.
examples: Schedule a dentist appointment for next week
When is my meeting with Sarah?
Move my 3pm call to tomorrow
Block out time for lunch on Friday
examples_generated: Schedule a dentist appointment for next week
When is my meeting with Sarah?
Move my 3pm call to tomorrow
Block out time for lunch on Friday

supported.description: Send messages, make calls, or manage contacts.
examples: Text Mom that I'm running late
Send a message to the team about the delay
Call the restaurant to make a reservation
What's John's phone number?
examples_generated: Text Mom that I'm running late
Send a message to the team about the delay
Call the restaurant to make a reservation
What's John's phone number?

supported.description: Get directions, check commute times, and location-based information.
examples: How long to get to the airport?
What's traffic like to downtown?
Find me a coffee shop nearby
Best route to avoid construction?
examples_generated: How long to get to the airport?
What's traffic like to downtown?
Find me a coffee shop nearby
Best route to avoid construction?

supported.description: Look up information, browse websites, and research topics online.
examples: Look up reviews for that new restaurant
Check if my flight is on time
Find articles about the latest iPhone
What's the weather forecast for this weekend?
examples_generated: Look up reviews for that new restaurant
Check if my flight is on time
Find articles about the latest iPhone
What's the weather forecast for this weekend?

tools.name: device.calendar.today
tools.description: Get all calendar events for today. Returns titles, times, locations, attendees, and notes. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.calendar.upcoming
tools.description: Get upcoming calendar events for the next N days (tomorrow and beyond). Does NOT include today. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

days.description: Number of days to look ahead (default 3, max 14)

tools.name: device.calendar.create
tools.description: Create a new calendar event.

tools.name: device.calendar.reschedule
tools.description: Reschedule an existing calendar event to a new date/time.

tools.name: device.calendar.cancel
tools.description: Cancel/delete a calendar event.

tools.name: device.contacts.search
tools.description: Search contacts by name, relationship, or keyword. Searches across name, relationship, email, and notes.

query.description: Search query — matches name, relationship, email, notes

tools.name: device.contacts.get
tools.description: Get full contact details by name or ID. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if contact data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.contacts.birthdays
tools.description: Get contacts with upcoming birthdays in the next N days. Returns matching contacts with days until birthday.

days.description: Look ahead N days for birthdays (default 30)

tools.name: device.contacts.lastContact
tools.description: Get when you last communicated with a contact. Returns days since last message/call. Useful for relationship care insights.

tools.name: device.location.current
tools.description: Get user's current location based on GPS. Returns place name, address, and coordinates. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.weather.current
tools.description: Get current weather conditions for the user's location. Returns temperature, conditions, humidity, wind, and UV index.

tools.name: device.weather.forecast
tools.description: Get weather forecast for the next N days. Returns daily highs, lows, conditions, and rain probability.

days.description: Number of days to forecast (default 3, max 7)

tools.name: device.commute.estimate
tools.description: Get estimated commute/travel time between two locations. Returns duration in minutes and distance. Uses Nominatim (geocoding) + OSRM (routing) — both free, no API key.

tools.name: device.battery
tools.description: Get device battery level and charging status. Response includes _freshness with last_sync_at, data_age_human, and is_stale (true if reading is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.connectivity
tools.description: Get device network connectivity status — WiFi, cellular, or offline. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.health.today
tools.description: Get today's health metrics from Apple Health: steps, floors climbed, active calories burned (kcal), and walking/running distance (km). Use for activity check-ins, daily progress, and pairing with nutrition data. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.health.history
tools.description: Get daily health metrics for the last N days (1-30) from Apple Health. Returns an array of per-day records with steps, floors, active calories, and distance — newest first (index 0 = today). Use for trends, weekly summaries, and feeding the nutrition tracker. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

days.description: How many days of history (1-30, default 30). Newest-first.

tools.name: device.message.send
tools.description: Send an SMS/iMessage to a contact. Queues the message for the phone to send. Always confirm with user before sending.

tools.name: device.notifications.list
tools.description: List device notifications. Optionally filter to unread only. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

unread_only.description: If true, return only unread notifications (default false)

tools.name: device.notifications.group
tools.description: Group notifications by a category: 'app', 'sender', or 'priority'. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

group_by.description: How to group: by app, sender, or priority

tools.name: device.notifications.dismiss
tools.description: Dismiss notifications by ID or by app name.

notification_id.description: Dismiss a single notification by ID

app.description: Dismiss all notifications from this app

tools.name: device.dnd.get
tools.description: Get current Do Not Disturb status — enabled/disabled, schedule, and allowlist. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: device.dnd.set
tools.description: Update Do Not Disturb settings. Toggle on/off, change schedule, or update allowlist.

allowlist.description: Contacts who can break through DND

tools.name: device.navigation.start
tools.description: Start navigation to a destination. Opens the maps app on the phone with directions.

tools.name: device.app.recent
tools.description: Get recently used apps and screen time. Returns app names, last used time, and usage duration. Response includes _freshness (is_stale=true if data is over 10 minutes old).

tools.name: integrations.list
tools.description: List all available external integrations (email, WhatsApp, etc.) with their tool names for status check and setup. Call this BEFORE using any external channel to discover what's available and how to connect it. The orchestrator should call each integration's status_tool to check connection, and setup_tool to guide user through setup if not connected.

tools.name: integrations.check
tools.description: Check a specific integration by channel name. Returns the integration details and which tools to call for status/setup. Use this when you already know which channel is needed.

tools.name: memory.store
tools.description: Store a long-term HUMAN/AGENT CONTEXT memory — facts, preferences, taught rules, user profile fields. Content must be concise, durable, and recallable. Types: preference, fact, instruction, pattern, rule, user_model; the engine assigns the CoALA kind automatically (semantic / procedural). DO NOT use for: session cookies, OAuth tokens, API keys, or any credentials (use platform.dataStore). DO NOT use for: raw tool-output dumps, large JSON blobs, or cached API responses (use platform.dataStore). The engine runs quality guards, dedup, normalize, and conflict detection; rejections include a `guidance` field telling you the correct tool/path. Returns { memory, conflicts, processing }.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

content.description: Memory content (natural language or JSON for structured types)

tools.name: memory.recall
tools.description: Recall memories by keyword query. Searches content + raw_content + tags. When embeddings come online this transparently becomes semantic.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.list
tools.description: List stored memories with optional type/kind filter and pagination.

tools.description: Update an existing memory by ID.

tools.name: memory.compact
tools.description: Run a compaction sweep — decay stale auto-learned memories and surface duplicate clusters. Does not auto-merge in P1; returns candidates for review.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: memory.supersede

tools.name: memory.embed

tools.name: memory.extract
tools.description: Run end-of-job auto-extraction over a transcript. Cheap regex pass always runs; smart LLM pass runs if smart=true and Core LLM is reachable. Writes extracted memories with source='auto' and lower confidence.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

smart.description: Also run LLM pass (default false in P1)

tools.name: gmail.move

to.description: Target label/folder name or ID (e.g. "Projects", "STARRED", or a custom label name). Fuzzy-matched against existing labels.

from.description: Source label/folder name or ID to remove. Defaults to "INBOX" if omitted. Also fuzzy-matched.

create.description: If true, create the target label when it does not exist. Default false — returns suggestions for similar labels instead.

tools.name: gmail.calendar.upcoming
tools.description: List upcoming events from the user's primary Google Calendar (next N hours). Returns normalized events with id, title, location, start_iso, end_iso, all_day, attendees, conference_uri (for video calls), and source="google_calendar". Requires calendar.readonly scope — if the user connected Gmail before this scope existed, they need to reconnect via platform.auth.ensureConnected({service_id:"gmail"}).

_adas_actor.description: Actor ID (injected by platform)

tools.name: calendar.list_events
tools.description: List Google Calendar events in a time window, optionally filtered by a free-text query. Prefers google-calendar service tokens; falls back to gmail token (calendar.readonly) for read access. Returns normalized events. If neither service is connected, returns connected:false — the planner should call platform.auth.ensureConnected({service_id:"google-calendar"}).

_adas_actor.description: Actor ID (injected by platform)

tools.name: calendar.create_event
tools.description: Create a new Google Calendar event. Requires google-calendar service connection (full Calendar scope). For all-day events set all_day=true and pass start/end as date strings (YYYY-MM-DD). For timed events pass ISO datetimes (e.g. "2026-05-04T14:00:00-04:00") and optionally a time_zone.

start.description: ISO datetime, or YYYY-MM-DD when all_day=true.

end.description: ISO datetime, or YYYY-MM-DD when all_day=true.

all_day.description: If true, treat start/end as date-only.

time_zone.description: IANA TZ (e.g. "America/New_York"). Optional when start/end carry an offset.

_adas_actor.description: Actor ID (injected by platform)

tools.name: calendar.update_event
tools.description: Patch an existing Google Calendar event. Only fields you pass are updated; others are left untouched. Requires google-calendar service connection.

start.description: ISO datetime or YYYY-MM-DD when all_day=true.

end.description: ISO datetime or YYYY-MM-DD when all_day=true.

_adas_actor.description: Actor ID (injected by platform)

tools.name: calendar.delete_event
tools.description: Delete (cancel) a Google Calendar event. Requires google-calendar service connection. Use send_updates="all" to email cancellations to attendees.

_adas_actor.description: Actor ID (injected by platform)

tools.name: gmail.cleanup
tools.description: Bulk cleanup: find ALL messages matching a Gmail search query and trash or archive them in one shot. Much faster than fetch+trash loops — processes up to 500 messages per call. Use for cleaning junk mail, newsletters, old notifications, etc. Returns total count + sample subjects for confirmation. Example queries: "from:noreply@example.com", "subject:newsletter older_than:30d", "category:promotions".

query.description: Gmail search query (same syntax as gmail.fetch filter). Required — cannot be empty to prevent accidental mass deletion.

action.description: What to do with matching messages. Default: trash.

_adas_actor.description: Actor ID (injected by platform)

tools.name: web.navigate

Opens a real headless browser (Playwright/Chromium), loads the page fully (JavaScript rendered), and returns a screenshot + page title. Capabilities: web search, read news headlines, check prices, visit social media profiles, access web applications, scrape website content, look up people/companies online, check stock prices, read articles, monitor web pages. Maintains login sessions and cookies.

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

tools.name: web.awaitLoginWebView
tools.description: [Internal] Block until the login WebView delivers cookies. Pair with web.openLoginWebView.

tools.name: web.openOAuthFlow
tools.description: Open an OAuth authorization URL in a user-driven WebView. When the user approves, the plugin detects the redirect URL matching `redirect_url_pattern`, extracts the OAuth params (default: `code`, `state`) from it, and calls the specified callback tool on the specified connector — e.g. `dropbox._storeTokens` on `dropbox-mcp`. No OAuth codes or tokens pass through the LLM. The callback URL can be any URL you've registered with the OAuth provider; it doesn't need to serve anything (404 is fine — the plugin only reads the URL, not the response body).

_adas_actor.description: Internal: actor ID injected by Core

auth_url.description: Full OAuth2 authorize URL including client_id, redirect_uri, state, scopes, etc. The calling connector is responsible for building this URL with a nonce-based state.

redirect_url_pattern.description: Regex matched against the current browser URL to detect callback. Example: 'dropbox/callback' or '/oauth/return'. Keep it broad enough to match regardless of host (your Dropbox-registered redirect_uri).

callback_tool.description: Tool to invoke on success, e.g. 'dropbox._storeTokens'. The plugin calls it with the extracted params as arguments: { code, state, ... }.

callback_connector.description: Connector id hosting the callback tool, e.g. 'dropbox-mcp'.

extract_params.description: Which URL query params to extract and pass to the callback tool. Default: ['code', 'state'].

label.description: Display label shown in the auth plugin (e.g. 'Dropbox'). Default: 'Sign in'.

tools.name: web.submitLoginWebView
tools.description: [Internal] Client-plugin callback delivering cookies. Not for planner use.

tools.name: triggers.list
tools.description: List all triggers (static + dynamic) for this tenant

_adas_tenant.description: Injected by platform — do not set manually

_adas_actor.description: Injected by platform — do not set manually

tools.name: triggers.toggle
tools.description: Pause or resume a specific trigger

skillSlug.description: Skill slug that owns the trigger

_adas_actor.description: Injected by platform

tools.name: meeting.commute.prep
tools.description: Twice-daily meeting commute prep. Lists upcoming events, classifies which need driving alerts (one LLM call), estimates drive time, and creates one-shot 'leave now' triggers. Idempotent (stable trigger_ids - dedup). Returns { ok, scanned, scheduled, skipped, errors, connections }.

_adas_actor.description: Injected by platform

tools.name: triggers.deleteDynamic
tools.description: Delete a dynamic (user-created) trigger/reminder

_adas_actor.description: Injected by platform

tools.name: connections.list
tools.description: List external services + connection state for this actor (Gmail, WhatsApp, Dropbox, etc.).

_adas_actor.description: Injected by platform

tools.name: connections.disconnect
tools.description: Revoke an external service connection for this actor.

service_id.description: Service id (e.g. 'gmail', 'linkedin'). Use connections.list to see valid ids.

_adas_actor.description: Injected by platform

tools.name: rooms.list
tools.description: List all rooms with their devices and states

tools.name: entity.state
tools.description: Get the current state of a device

tools.name: services.call
tools.description: Toggle or control a device

tools.name: hue.status
tools.description: Get Philips Hue integration status

tools.name: tuya.status
tools.description: Get Tuya/Smart Life integration status

tools.name: google.status
tools.description: Get Google Home/Nest integration status

triggers.name: Meeting commute prep
triggers.description: Twice-daily — schedules a 'leave now' alert for every driving meeting in the next 36h
triggers.prompt: Call the meeting.commute.prep tool. Return its JSON result as a single line. No chat, no explanation, no questions.

Schedule trigger must have an "every" field (ISO8601 duration)
errors.suggestion: Use format like "PT2M" (2 minutes), "PT1H" (1 hour), "P1D" (1 day)
